description: Fail-closed private desktop bridge contract between bundled local Tauri assets and the packaged Haven 42 engine sidecar.

properties.requestId: 1-64 ASCII letters, digits, period, underscore, or hyphen; unique while active
properties.operationId: exact registered id resolved from the registry selected by operationKind
properties.mode: plan, execute, or apply; the operation policy may allow fewer modes
properties.input: operation-specific object validated against its registered schema
properties.pathGrantIds: optional array of unexpired native-issued path grant ids
properties.approvalTokenId: optional opaque native-issued token bound to this request and disclosed effects
properties.includeOutput: optional boolean; false by default because output may contain local data

rules: cancelRequestId must identify an active request owned by the same desktop session
cancellation is best-effort and must produce one terminal result or error event
cancellation cannot select or signal an arbitrary operating-system process

pathGrants.issuedBy: native bridge after user-driven native selection or an application-owned path decision
rules: grant ids are opaque, session-bound, expiring, and non-transferable
the native bridge canonicalizes every target before access
path traversal, root escape, disallowed symlink or reparse-point escape, and grant-type mismatch are rejected
a read grant never implies write permission
application, engine, update, rollback, credential, and private-key directories cannot be user-granted

tokenRules: tokens are opaque, single-use, session-bound, short-lived, and stored only in native memory
tokens bind requestId, operationId, mode, disclosed effects, selected path grants, and expiry
changed input, effects, operation, grants, session, or expiry invalidates the token

privacy.notes: Renderer messages, prompts, paths, endpoints, provider output, and artifact content remain local and are minimized. Sanitized evidence is an explicit separate action.

failureRules: reject malformed JSON, oversized lines, invalid UTF-8, unsupported schema versions, duplicate active request ids, and additional properties
reject unknown operation ids before examining operation input
reject direct commands, executable paths, arbitrary arguments, raw filesystem paths, URLs, and endpoints
reject missing, expired, reused, mismatched, or over-broad grants and approvals
terminate the sidecar session after repeated framing violations without attempting partial recovery
